title: linanthus californicus subsp. tomentosus
intro: Linanthus californicus subsp. tomentosus, also known as Woolly Purple Sage, is a subspecies of flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Woolly Purple Sage include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Woolly Purple Sage can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
